purpose: Defines the master agent's context-efficiency discipline — the eleven principles for gathering project context with minimal reads while preserving context window space for reasoning. Covers Glob/Grep first (zero-token cost, always current), CLAUDE.md for orientation, project TOC for semantic discovery, read-with-purpose, parallel reads, targeted line ranges, cache awareness post-compaction, summarize-don't-hoard, ~8000-token worker prompt budget, filtered convention injection, and sibling communication for context savings. Also documents the {project_root}/.synapse/ project metadata directory (toc.md, config.json, fingerprints.json, dep_graph.json) and includes the canonical Synapse directory structure.
gotchas: Glob/Grep cost ZERO context tokens and are always current — use them BEFORE reaching for the TOC. Reaching for TOC first when filenames reveal purpose wastes tokens.
After context compaction, the agent loses file contents from earlier reads — re-read critical files rather than working from stale memory.

Worker prompt budget is ~8000 tokens TOTAL — bloated prompts are the primary cause of worker context exhaustion. Filter conventions by relevance: a backend worker doesn't need frontend styling conventions.
For large CLAUDE.md (500+ lines), always SUMMARIZE rather than quote when injecting into worker prompts.
Sibling shared_context can substitute for redundant file reads — workers in the same wave can read each other's progress files instead of re-reading source files a peer already processed.
Reading 'just in case' is wasteful — every read should have a known expected outcome before invoking it.
{project_root}/.synapse/ is OPTIONAL — Synapse works without it but is more context-efficient when it exists. The directory should be added to project's .gitignore.
patterns: Discovery hierarchy: Glob/Grep > CLAUDE.md > TOC > full file reads — escalate only when needed.
Parallel multi-file reads: never read files sequentially when they have no dependency on each other.
Targeted line ranges via offset for large files — read the section you need, not the whole file.
Worker prompt section budgeting: split into description/context/conventions/upstream results/instructions, estimate each, split task or summarize if total exceeds 8000 tokens.
Sibling-context substitution: prefer reading sibling shared_context over re-reading the same source files.
conventions: {project_root}/.synapse/ contents: toc.md (semantic index), config.json (project-Synapse config), fingerprints.json (semantic fingerprints from !toc_generate), dep_graph.json (file-level deps).
TOC generation threshold: consider !toc_generate for projects with 500+ files.
